channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global Geopolitical News Headlines - Tier 1 Sources

item.title: Pakistan Accused of Bombing Kunar University in Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistani air strikes on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and wounded 75, including university students. Taliban condemned the attack as 'war crimes,' with reports of jets, drones, and mortars targeting civilian areas.

item.title: Suspect in Washington Dinner Shooting Charged With Attempted Assassination of Trump
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump after opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. He faces life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Indonesia Train Collision Kills 14 Near Jakarta
item.description: A long-distance train crashed into a commuter train outside Jakarta, killing at least 14 people and injuring 84. Rescue teams worked to free passengers trapped in the women-only carriage. The accident raises fresh concerns about Indonesia's aging rail infrastructure.

item.title: Lithuania Arrests 13 Over Russian GRU-Linked Murder Plots
item.description: Lithuanian authorities charged 13 people with attempted murders linked to Russia's GRU military intelligence. The network allegedly targeted a Ukrainian fundraiser and a Russian dissident, with connections to sabotage in Bulgaria and espionage in Greece.

item.title: King Charles III Arrives at White House for State Visit Amid US-UK Tensions
item.description: King Charles III met President Trump at the White House for a four-day state visit aimed at repairing strained US-UK relations. The visit comes as Trump has criticized Prime Minister Starmer over Britain's refusal to join US military action against Iran.

item.title: Trump Unhappy With Latest Iranian Proposal, US Official Says
item.description: President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's latest offer to end the two-month war, as Tehran's proposal did not address nuclear program concerns. Peace talks remain stalled with no breakthrough in sight.

item.title: US and Iran Clash at UN Over Nuclear Non-Proliferation Role
item.description: Tensions flared at the United Nations after Tehran was selected as a vice president for the NPT review conference, prompting a sharp rebuke from Washington over Iran's nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: NATO Considers Ending Annual Summits Amid Trump Tensions
item.description: Six sources told Reuters that NATO is weighing whether to end its practice of annual summits, potentially avoiding a tense encounter with Trump in his final year as questions mount over the alliance's future.

item.title: China Denounces Japan and EU at UN Over South China Sea
item.description: Beijing lashed out at Tokyo and Brussels during a UN Security Council maritime security meeting, accusing Japan of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and military expansion plans.

item.title: Russian Drone Strike on Odesa Wounds 14 Including Children
item.description: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern port city wounded over a dozen people, including two children, and damaged residential buildings as the war continues.

item.title: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030
item.description: UNICEF warns that Taliban restrictions on girls education and women employment could eliminate 25% of Afghanistan 2021 female workforce, affecting 1 million girls currently with projections to double by 2030.

item.title: Regional police crackdown arrests 326 in Asia-wide child abuse probe
item.description: Singapore, Brunei, Hong Kong, Japan, Malaysia, South Korea and Thailand conducted a four-week operation arresting 326 people and investigating 119 more across 382 locations for online child sexual exploitation.

item.title: Zelensky orders 50,000 ground robots for Ukraine military
item.description: Ukraine president announces major expansion of unmanned ground systems for logistics, evacuations and combat, following widespread drone adoption as the next step in modern warfare.

item.title: Vietnam gas giant shifts LPG imports from Middle East to US amid Iran war
item.description: PetroVietnam Gas will import 66,000 tonnes of cooking fuel from US in May versus 44,000 from Middle East, as Iran war disrupts Hormuz Strait and forces Asian buyers to find new suppliers.

item.title: Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Board
item.description: All 22 members of the National Science Board, the independent body overseeing the National Science Foundation, received termination notices from the Presidential Personnel Office. Scientists warn the move could enable deep cuts to fundamental research funding.

item.title: Pakistan Accused of Airstrikes on Afghan University
item.description: Taliban officials say Pakistani attacks on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and injured 75, including students at Kunar University. Pakistan denied the reports. The attacks threaten a fragile ceasefire brokered by China in April.

item.title: Taiwan on alert as Chinese warships approach Penghu Islands
item.description: Taiwan detected a Chinese destroyer and frigate operating near the Penghu islands, home to major Taiwanese military bases, prompting Taipei to deploy naval and air forces to monitor the vessels.

item.title: German Chancellor links Ukraine peace to territorial concessions
item.description: Friedrich Merz suggested Ukraine may need to cede territory to Russia as part of a peace deal, framing such concessions as a potential pathway to EU membership for Kyiv.

item.title: North Korea opens memorial for troops killed in Ukraine war
item.description: Kim Jong Un inaugurated a memorial museum honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, pledging continued support for Moscow's war effort during a ceremony attended by Russia's defense minister.

item.title: Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter
item.description: Pope Leo XIV prayed with Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally at the Vatican, vowing to overcome differences in a historic meeting with the first female leader of the Church of England and the global Anglican Communion.

item.title: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran support
item.description: Bahrain revoked citizenship of 69 people for what it called sympathy with Iran's hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities. Rights groups condemned the move as a 'blatant abuse of power'.

item.title: Putin Pledges Support for Iran in St. Petersburg Talks with Foreign Minister Araghchi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg, offering support to navigate the "difficult period" while expressing hope for peace. Moscow has resisted being drawn into the Middle East conflict while continuing its invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Lebanese President Aoun Rejects "Wars Serving Foreign Interests" in Rebuke to Hezbollah
item.description: President Joseph Aoun pushed back against critics of negotiations with Israel, declaring that "traitors are those who drag Lebanon into a war to serve foreign interests" — a direct criticism of Hezbollah's wars "in support of Gaza and Iran" rather than Lebanon's interests.

item.title: Australia Coalition Proposes $800M Plan to Double Fuel Reserves
item.description: Australia's federal Coalition unveiled an $800 million plan to double the nation's minimum fuel reserves to 60 days, citing energy security concerns amid the Middle East conflict. The proposal adds at least one billion litres of new onshore storage with a focus on diesel.

item.title: US reviews latest Iranian proposal to end war stalemate
item.description: President Trump discussed a new Iranian proposal with his national security team as Tehran's foreign minister headed to Russia. The proposal reportedly offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the US blockade.
